title: Administrative Business Partner
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

Wherever Waymo works, Business Support teams are on the job. We're the partners who plan events, work with external partners, and orchestrate the daily activities of our teams and leaders, the real estate specialists who find and build amazing workspaces and research facilities; the systems experts who develop and run our intuitive IT and business tools. Together, we do everything it takes to keep Waymo rolling.

In this hybrid role, you will report into Executive Team Leadership.

You will:

  • Independently execute essential administrative functions, including travel, expenses, confidential calendar management, and facilities coordination with limited guidance
  • Proactively solve problems, exercising sound judgment and discretion even with limited information
  • Collaborate effectively and build trust with executives, cross-functional partners (e.g., HR, Finance, Chief of Staff), and teams across the organization to achieve complex objectives
  • Utilize strong communication and interpersonal skills to build rapport, influence collaboration, and engage effectively with diverse internal and external stakeholders
  • Lead and deliver medium-to-large scale projects or events from initiation through completion
  • Influence collaboration between Waymonauts and build rapport, positively impacting team velocity

You have:

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience
  • 5+ years of experience providing direct executive support, project management, or event planning to senior leaders (VP+) within a fast-paced technology or startup environment
  • Experience managing complex scheduling and logistics with external partners, demonstrating responsiveness, flexibility, and sound judgment with confidentiality.
  • Exceptional organizational, time management, and prioritization skills with the ability to manage multiple complex tasks in a dynamic setting.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills: Proven ability to build strong relationships, collaborate effectively across diverse teams/functions, and maintain strict confidentiality.
  • Strong business acumen, analytical problem-solving skills, and the ability to adapt effectively to different leadership styles and partnership dynamics.

We prefer:

  • Tech Proficiency: Demonstrated ability to leverage technology for increased efficiency and quickly adopt new tools.
  • Project Leadership & Operational Problem Solving: Proven ability to lead projects, manage processes, and independently resolve operational issues through strategic thinking and data analysis.

Travel Requirement: Occasional travel, while rare, might be needed to support a combination of partnership and internal onsite support at other locations (domestic and international).

The expected hourly range for this position is listed below. Actual starting pay will be based on job-related factors, including exact work location, experience, relevant training and education, and skill level.  Waymo employees are also eligible to participate in Waymo’s discretionary annual bonus program, equity incentive plan, and generous Company benefits program, subject to eligibility requirements.

Hourly Range
$51.45—$64.91 USD

title: Analysis Infra SWE
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

You will:

  • Develop and maintain a data processing platform used by onboard and offboard teams to extract signals from petabytes of driving logs and simulation traces.
  • Design and build low-latency streaming data processing platform, enabling real-time identification of critical driving events.
  • Improve the reliability, scalability, and efficiency of our signal generation infrastructure, establishing SLOs and reducing operational toil for massive-scale data processing jobs.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional partners in Perception, Planner, and Data Science to migrate legacy pipelines to modern infrastructure and support new analytical use cases.

You have:

  • BS or MS in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or equivalent 
  • 5+ years of professional experience in backend engineering, databases, and production monitoring (3+ years for L4, and relevant internship for L3)
  • Experience in leading projects and driving collaborating with cross-functional teams (for L4 and L3, this is not required and can be moved to preferred below)
  • Experience with general-purpose programming languages, especially C++.

  • Experience  Comment start with capacity planning, resource forecasting models, and cost attribution strategies for high-throughput or streaming data processing infrastructure. Comment end  

  • Excellent collaboration and communication skills

We prefer:

  • Familiarity with distributed processing frameworks (e.g. Hadoop, Flume, and Spark) with stream processing frameworks (e.g., Flink, Flume Streaming, Kafka).
  • Proven track record of driving collaboration effectively across organizational boundaries, building multi-dimensional stakeholder relationships, and importing and exporting ideas to achieve broad organizational goals
  • Familiarity with infrastructure-as-code and reliability engineering practices (SLOs, error budgets)

title: ASIC Design Verification Engineer
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

Waymo's Compute Team is tasked with a critical and exciting mission: We deliver the compute platform responsible for running the fully autonomous vehicle’s software stack. To achieve our mission, we architect and create high-performance custom silicon; we develop system-level compute architectures that push the boundaries of performance, power, and latency; and we collaborate closely with many other teammates to ensure we design and optimize hardware and software for maximum performance. We are a multidisciplinary team seeking curious and talented teammates to work on one of the world’s highest performance automotive compute platforms.

This role follows a hybrid work schedule and you will report to a Silicon Engineering Lead

You will:

  • Partner with design and architecture teams to translate hardware specifications into comprehensive, scalable verification plans
  • Drive the development of testbenches, reference models, and stimulus to validate mission-critical functionality and performance
  • Architect and enhance verification environments, contributing to shared tools and reusable methodologies across the organization
  • Evaluate, integrate, and verify third-party Verification IP (VIP) to accelerate the development cycle
  • Define and analyze coverage metrics (functional and code) to ensure design readiness and closure
  • Advocate and establish verification best-practices

 

You have:

  • 3+ years of experience building and maintaining complex testbenches using UVM/SystemVerilog
  • Proven track record with constrained-random generation, functional coverage, and SVA (SystemVerilog Assertions)
  • Deep understanding of complex digital logic and the ability to debug intricate hardware/software interactions
  • Proficiency in Python for developing scalable automation frameworks, data analysis tools, and regression management suites
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams
  • Strong analytical skills in root-causing failures across RTL, testbench, and environment layers

 

We prefer:

  • Experience spanning initial specification through post-silicon bring-up and validation
  • Familiarity with power-aware verification (UPF), formal verification, or hardware-software co-validation
  • Domain expertise in ML accelerators, high-speed interconnects (NoCs), or high-bandwidth memory
  • Experience with C/C++ for reference model development and enhancement
  • Hands-on experience with industry-standard interfaces such as PCIe Gen 5/6, DDR5, or Ethernet
  • Interest or experience in leveraging Generative AI and LLMs to accelerate verification workflows, such as automated testbench generation, documentation, or failure log analysis

 

The expected base salary range for this full-time position across US locations is listed below. Actual starting pay will be based on job-related factors, including exact work location, experience, relevant training and education, and skill level. We are looking for a passionate Staff SWE who wants to help build the systems that manage and increase Waymo's core ride hailing and fleet management systems. If you're someone who's curious and passionate about Level 4 autonomous driving, proactive and product-minded and enjoy working on cross-cutting problems that require cross-team collaboration, both technically (mobile app, cloud backends, and the Waymo Driver) and functionally (operations, product, legal, partnerships, policy & privacy) to develop and deliver business critical infrastructure, Then we'd like to meet you!

title: Brand Marketing Manager, Celebrity & Influencer
location: Mountain View, CA, USA; San Francisco, CA, USA
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

On the Waymo Marketing team, you will help to connect people with the magic and impact of Waymo's autonomous driving technology. Our team has the unique opportunity to not only grow Waymo's brand from the ground up, but to also build societal acceptance for autonomous driving technology for the first time in history. We build thoughtful marketing strategy, positioning, messaging and creative assets to deliver messages that matter to our audience, communicated through anything from video content, to social media interactions, to immersive physical experiences. We also shape Waymo's go-to-market initiatives, helping to transform Waymo into a thriving business with a growing user base.

This role follows a hybrid work schedule and reports to our Brand & Culture Marketing Lead.

You will:

  • Lead and manage the end-to-end earned and paid strategy for Waymo’s influencer and celebrity talent programs aligning with broader brand and culture marketing objectives and driving breakthrough performance alongside our core agency partners
  • Identify and cultivate deep relationships with creators, celebrity talent, management, and talent agencies to grow Waymo’s network of influential voices.
  • Develop creative campaigns that tap the star power of influencer and celebrity figures to fuel Waymo’s mission to be the World’s Most Trusted Driver.
  • Negotiate and execute complex talent contracts, securing competitive terms, usage rights, and best-in-class deliverables.
  • Establish measurement frameworks to deliver meaningful performance insights and track the impact of talent partnerships on brand sentiment, user acquisition, and cultural share-of-voice.
  • Act as the internal subject matter expert, educating stakeholders on how to effectively leverage talent to meet business goals and staying ahead of the curve on emerging platforms, trends, and shifts in the celebrity and creator economy to keep Waymo at the forefront of culture.

You have:

  • 7+ years of experience in influencer marketing, talent management, or celebrity partnerships within a high-growth consumer brand or top-tier agency.
  • An extensive "Rolodex" of direct contacts at major talent agencies, management teams and established relationships within the creator community.
  • Proven track record of executing best-in-class influencer programs and high-profile celebrity campaigns that broke through the noise and drove measurable cultural impact.
  • Exceptional negotiation skills, with a deep understanding of SAG-AFTRA regulations, licensing, and digital talent usage rights.
  • Data-driven mindset, with a strong skillset in analyzing campaign performance metrics, translating data into compelling reports, and deriving insights to inform optimization strategies.
  • A cultural pulse on "the next big thing"—you know who is trending and what content consumers are leaning into before they go viral.

We prefer:

  • Experience working specifically with technology or mobility brands, translating complex product features into consumer-friendly narratives.
  • Background working at an influencer marketing or talent agency and/or in-house on a brand’s influencer or talent marketing team.
  • Experience managing external influencer and celebrity marketing agencies.
  • Global talent expertise with experience managing talent partnerships across the US, EMEA and APAC.
  • Familiarity with PR and Earned Media, knowing how to turn talent partnerships into headline-grabbing moments.
  • A genuine passion for autonomous technology and a forward-thinking vision for how it improves urban mobility

Travel Requirements: 

  • 1-3 times per quarter

description_text:

We also shape Waymo's go-to-market initiatives, helping to transform Waymo into a thriving business with a growing user base.

In this hybrid role, you will report to a Brand Media & Analytics Lead.

You will:

  • Lead the strategy and deployment of high-impact brand media across digital (CTV, programmatic video, paid social) and high-visibility offline channels (linear TV, OOH, cinema, local sponsorships) to build market-wide familiarity.
  • Partner on full-funnel media strategy alongside the Growth team ensuring brand media flights create a high-intent audience pipeline that performance marketing can efficiently retarget and convert into active riders.
  • Manage and track strategic allocation of brand media budget balancing upper-funnel brand health goals with lower-funnel acquisition needs to maximize overall marketing efficiency.
  • Guide agency partners in developing media plans that build brand awareness and trust while maintaining the agility precise targeting and frequency caps required for performance-adjacent scaling.
  • Collaborate with growth and data teams to build unified audience segmentation strategies using first-party data and brand lift insights to feed growth marketing lookalike models.
  • Work cross-functionally to map how upper-funnel brand investments directly impact downstream growth metrics, including foundational MMM (Mixed media modeling).

You have:

  • 7+ years of experience managing large-scale paid media campaigns with a proven track record of successfully running brand-building initiatives that directly accelerate growth marketing velocity.
  • Direct experience working hand-in-hand with growth performance or lifecycle marketing teams to pass audiences seamlessly through the consumer journey from awareness to first ride.
  • Deep operational knowledge of both brand-heavy mediums (Out-of-Home, linear/connected TV, audio) and highly targeted digital platforms (Meta, YouTube, TikTok, programmatic DSPs).
  • Proven success managing top-tier media planning and buying agencies with the ability to ruthlessly prioritize deliverables negotiate inventory and maintain strict creative standards.
  • Strong fluency in brand health tracking (awareness consideration trust) coupled with a solid understanding of performance metrics (CAC LTV conversion rates and ROAS).
  • Exceptional communication skills with a track record of aligning creative teams, product marketing, growth and finance teams around a singular cohesive media narrative.

 

We prefer:

  • Prior experience driving integrated marketing for high-density localized marketplaces like ride-hailing micromobility or on-demand delivery networks.
  • Experience scaling brand media footprint into brand-new geographic territories where localized operational constraints require agile geo-fenced media deployment.
  • Familiarity with how Media Mix Modeling (MMM) Multi-Touch Attribution (MTA) and matched-market lift testing help prove the economic value of brand media on long-term performance.
  • An intuitive sense of how to use paid media to overcome consumer skepticism and mainstream a completely novel transit category.
  • Deep familiarity utilizing Google’s advanced enterprise tools (e.g. Display & Video 360 Ads Data Hub YouTube Select) to execute highly sophisticated brand campaigns.
  • Thrive in a rapidly evolving competitive and regulatory landscape with the capacity to pivot creative assets and media weights on short notice.

title: Business Continuity Charging Infrastructure Manager
location: Mountain View, CA, USA;  Washington D.C., USA
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

This role reports to our Charging Infrastructure Program Manager. 

 

You will:

  • Develop and refine engineering designs, specifications, and procedures to eliminate single points of failure across mechanical, electrical, and civil systems in critical facilities (e.g., service centers, vehicle chargers).
  • Perform rigorous Failure Modes and Effects Analysis (FMEA) and Root Cause Analysis (RCA) on existing and proposed systems to identify weaknesses and quantify risk, providing clear recommendations for mitigation.
  • Act as the subject matter expert to review all design documents, equipment specifications, construction submittals, and factory test results to ensure compliance with targeted redundancy topologies.
  • Create detailed Integrated Systems Testing and operational readiness procedures to validate the failover capabilities and resilience of complex, inter-dependent utility and infrastructure systems including Smart Building Automation. 
  • Ensure all redundancy designs and modifications adhere to relevant industry standards, codes (e.g., NFPA, ASHRAE, seismic codes), and internal reliability metrics and availability SLAs. Ensure all redundancy projects are completed within allocated time and budget. 
  • Collaborate with project managers and vendor partners to prioritize and scope capital improvement projects focused on redundancy upgrades, system modernization, and mitigating facility risks.

 

You have:

  • 6 + years of professional experience in designing, implementing, and commissioning mission-critical infrastructure, specifically within mechanical (HVAC, cooling systems), electrical power (UPS, switchgear, generators), and/or civil/structural systems.
  • Expert-level knowledge of diverse redundancy topologies (e.g., N, N+1, N+2, 2N, 2N+1) and the ability to design and validate systems ensuring target Service Level Agreements (SLAs) for uptime and availability.
  • Experience leading cross-functional engineering teams and external consultants, driving the redundancy review process from conceptual design through construction.
  • Thorough understanding of relevant building codes, industry standards (e.g., Uptime Institute Tiers, ANSI/TIA-942), and safety regulations related to critical power, fire suppression, and structural integrity.

 

We prefer:

  • Certification or Industry Designation in Project, Engineering or Facilities Services 
  • Experience in mixed use environments - office, industrial, labs or manufacturing  
  • Demonstrable PM skills; able to advance projects with undefined scope, influence and align stakeholders

 

Travel requirements:

  • This role requires travel approximately 35% of the time. Specifically, the employee will be expected to travel between Bay Area and Washington DC locations as part of their job responsibilities.

Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

The Business Development and Partnerships team helps plan Waymo's dynamic future and drive the company's go-to-market strategy. We work cross-functionally and leverage quantitative and qualitative analysis to build forward-looking partnerships that deliver on Waymo's strategic business objectives. As a member of this entrepreneurial and results-oriented team, you will have a significant impact on defining, developing and executing Waymo's commercial deployment.

In this hybrid role, you will report to a Head of Business Development & Strategic Partnerships, Commercial Growth.

ビジネス開発チームおよびパートナーシップ チームは、Waymo のダイナミックな未来構想と市場開拓戦略の推進を担っています。部門横断的に活動し、定量的および定性的な分析を駆使して、Waymo の戦略的なビジネス目標を達成するための将来を見据えたパートナーシップを構築します。起業家精神にあふれ、結果を重視する本チームの一員として、Waymo の商用デプロイを実現するための重要サービスの定義、開発、実行において大きな影響を持つ役割を担当していただきます。

 

You Will / 業務内容:

  • Develop and execute on a strategic partnerships strategy and plan to ensure successful go-to-market in Japan / 戦略的パートナーシップの立案と実行を通じて、日本における市場参入を成功に導く
  • Identify, create, negotiate and implement local strategic partnerships across the AV partnerships ecosystem, including operations and fleet partners, city and transit organizations, airports and other partners that support go-to-market, education/awareness, and growth/ 運用およびフリートのパートナー、自治体や交通機関、空港、さらには市場認知の向上や成長を支援する各団体など、自律運転エコシステム全般にわたる地域の戦略的パートナーシップを特定、構築し、交渉から実装を行う
  • Work closely with Waymo Policy & Government Affairs and Legal teams to help influence national and local regulatory frameworks to enable testing and commercial deployment / Waymo の政策および政府渉外チーム、法務チームと緊密に連携し、国や地域の規制の枠組みへの働きかけをサポートし、テストとおよび商用デプロイを可能にする
  • Can effectively represent Waymo in high stake external meetings with the government and strategic partners / 政府機関や戦略的パートナーとの重要な外部会議において、Waymo を代表して効果的な説明を行う
  • Act as a primary bridge between the Japan-based team and global teams at Waymo HQ, coordinating closely with partnerships efforts managed centrally and serving as a key voice representing local partnership interests, trends, and nuances.  Help Waymo achieve long term success in Japan with the most thoughtful approach - influence global thinking with a deep understanding of local nuances / 日本拠点と Waymo 本社のグローバル チームをつなぐ主要な窓口となり、本社が統括するパートナーシップ戦略と密に連携しつつ、国内におけるパートナーシップのニーズやトレンド、文化的背景を共有する。日本特有の市場環境や習慣への深い理解に基づき、グローバル戦略への反映を行うことで、Waymo が日本で長期的な成功を収めるための最適かつ配慮あるアプローチを支援する
  • Collaborate with teams across time zones to define strategy, solve complex problems, execute business initiatives, create work plans, lead analyses, develop and communicate final recommendations to move partnerships forward and ensure their ongoing success / 異なるタイムゾーンのチームと連携して戦略を定義し、複雑な課題解決やビジネス イニシアチブの実行、作業計画の策定から分析の主導を担う。また、パートナーシップを前進させて継続的な成功を確実にするための最終提言の作成および伝達を行う

You Have / 必要な条件/経験:

  • 8+ years of work experience, including 5+ years in business development or strategic partnerships in the technology, transportation and/or mobility sector / 8 年以上の実務経験。そのうち 5 年以上は、テクノロジー、運輸、モビリティ分野における事業開発または戦略的パートナーシップの領域の経験であること
  • Full business proficiency in both Japanese and English language (verbal and written) and the ability to cross-contextualize and communicate effectively across both Japanese and US cultures and styles / 日本語および英語における高いビジネス語学力（会話および読み書き）。日米両方の文化やスタイルの違いを深く理解し、文脈を補完しながら効果的にコミュニケーションを図る能力
  • Experience structuring and negotiating strategic partnership agreements / 戦略的パートナーシップ契約の構築および交渉における実務経験
  • Experience leading and influencing a cross-functional team, particularly a team that includes policy, operations, product, marketing and finance stakeholders / 部門横断的なチーム（特に政策、運用、プロダクト、マーケティング、財務などのステークホルダーを含むチーム）を牽引し、組織に影響を与えた経験
  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience / 学士号、またはそれに相当する実務経験

 

We Prefer / 望ましい条件/経験:

  • Deep understanding of the autonomous vehicle, ride hailing or broader mobility industries / 自律運転車、配車サービス、または幅広いモビリティ業界における深い知識
  • Deep understanding of the Japanese business landscape, culture, and regulatory environment (especially in mobility or technology) / 日本のビジネス環境、文化、および規制環境（特にモビリティやテクノロジー分野）に関する深い知識
  • Experience working in both US and Japanese companies / 米国企業および日本企業双方における勤務経験
  • Direct experience with international market entry, product launches, or scaling a new business / 国際市場への参入、プロダクト リリース、または新規事業の拡大に直接携わった経験

 

Provide travel requirements (if applicable) / 出張について（該当する場合）:

  • Travel will be required, around 10-20% of time / 業務時間の10〜20% 程度の出張が発生する場合があります

title: Business Systems Lead - Insurance & Operations
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

The Business Systems team defines and builds the business IT architecture that supports the commercial operation of Waymo's autonomous fleet. The team stands at the intersection of finance, operations and IT; through creative architecture, expertise and attention to detail, it ensures that Waymo has world-class capabilities across all its business processes.

In this hybrid role, you will report to Sr Manager Supply Chain, GTM & Data Intelligence Systems

 

You will:

  • Own the comprehensive technology strategy and define the solution roadmap across the entire portfolio: Operations and Insurance, ensuring a phased implementation approach aligned with strategic business objectives.
  • Manage the full system lifecycle, including the setup, advanced integration, configuration, and continuous operational support of core platforms such as Risk and Insurance systems (e.g., Origami and TPA), LMS/Content management platforms, and other Operations tools.
  • Direct configuration, customization, and integration efforts across systems to ensure seamless data flow and system interoperability between platforms (e.g., Origami, LMS,Brightspot, Salesforce, Tulip, Fleet Management tools), enabling scalable, reliable, and efficient business operations.
  • Drive and govern end-to-end functional excellence, leading all phases of the project lifecycle including process re-engineering, requirements definition, data mapping, fit/gap analysis, solution design, testing, training, reporting, production support, and continuous improvement across Insurance and Operations ecosystems.
  • Oversee the creation and standardization of all necessary 
  • project documentation: Business Requirements Documents (BRD), Functional Specification Documents (FSD), Technical Specifications, process flow diagrams, user stories, and test cases.
  • Serve as the primary cross-functional leader, managing key stakeholder relationships and driving successful product and/or feature delivery, organizational alignment, and change management.

You have:

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent years of work experience.

  • Minimum 12+ years of progressive experience in Business Systems Analysis, with substantial hands-on and leadership experience across Risk & Insurance, LMS/Content Management platforms, and Operations with multiple full lifecycle implementations.

  • Exceptional executive-level communication, negotiation, and cross-functional leadership skills proven in a complex, fast-paced organizational environment.

We prefer:

  • Advanced degree (e.g., MBA or equivalent).

  • Relevant professional certifications.

  • Expertise in enterprise architecture, integrations, and large-scale data management.

The expected base salary range for this full-time position across US locations is listed below. Actual starting pay will be based on job-related factors, including exact work location, experience, relevant training and education, and skill level. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the role location or, if the role can be performed remote, the specific salary range for your preferred location, during the hiring process. 

Waymo employees are also eligible to participate in Waymo’s discretionary annual bonus program, equity incentive plan, and generous Company benefits program, subject to eligibility requirements. 

Salary Range
$200,000—$247,000 USD

title: Central Production and Performance Sr. Manager
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

Waymo Operations exists to deliver the Waymo Driver to the world. We are a global team building and scaling the world's first and leading autonomous fleet and operations platform. From component sourcing to end customer management, we enable and create value for Waymo through scaled and orchestrated deployment of the Waymo Driver. At Waymo, we are dedicated to building a culture that promotes collaboration and celebration. We value our team members' unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences and support and encourage all team members to share their ideas to help Waymo better serve the communities in which we operate./ Waymo Operations の目的は、Waymo Driver を世界中にお届けすることです。私たちは世界初かつ最先端の自律走行車両と、その運用プラットフォームの構築および拡大を担っているグローバル チームです。部品の調達から顧客の管理まで、Waymo Driver を大規模かつ緻密に展開することで Waymo の価値創造を行っています。また、Waymo ではお互いに協力し合い、称え合う文化を築いています。チームメンバーそれぞれのバックグラウンドや視点、経験を尊重し、Waymo が事業を展開するコミュニティへさらに貢献するため、全員が自由にアイデアを提案できるようサポートし、奨励しています。

You will:/業務内容: 

  • Local Market Leadership and Partner Support: As the first operations hire in Japan, ensure our Fleet Operating Partner is well supported by facilitating in-person touchpoints, feedback loops, and internal advocacy to operations and engineering teams to unblock their issues and empower their success./ ローカル マーケットにおけるリーダーシップとパートナー支援: 日本におけるオペレーション部門の最初のメンバーとして、フリート オペレーション パートナーを手厚くサポートする。対面でのタッチポイント、フィードバック ループの構築、社内のオペレーション チームとエンジニアリング チームへの働きかけを通じて、パートナーの課題を解決し、その成功を支援する。
  • Global Daily Operations Monitoring and Support: Actively oversee daily autonomous vehicle operations across all markets in real time. Monitor live operational data to detect and respond to immediate issues impacting AV performance. Investigate the “why” when issues occur, escalating to and collaborating with local teams and global operations for timely intervention and with product / engineering for long term solutions./グローバルな日常業務の監視とサポート: 全市場における自律運転車の日常運用をリアルタイムで積極的に監視する。ライブ運用データをモニタリングして、車両のパフォーマンスに影響を与えるような緊急を要する課題を検出し、対応を行う。問題発生時にはその「原因（なぜ）」を調査し、現地チームおよびグローバル オペレーションにエスカレーションしてタイムリーな介入を行うとともに、プロダクト / エンジニアリング チームと連携して長期的な解決策を策定する。
  • Performance Management & Support: Directly manage performance for global commercial and testing operations, while providing hands-on guidance and real-time coaching to Waymo’s partners and Partners./ パフォーマンス管理とサポート: グローバルな商用運用およびテスト業務におけるパフォーマンスを直接管理する。同時に Waymo のパートナーや提携先に、実践的なガイダンスとリアルタイムのコーチングを提供する。
  • Real-Time Reporting and Communication: Regularly update key stakeholders on team activities, operational status, and any incidents. Ensure accurate and prompt communication with global operations and other teams for effective coordination and rapid response. Manage complex stakeholder relationships across multiple teams, including local operations, systems operations, product development, engineering, and others./ リアルタイム レポーティングおよびコミュニケーション: チームの活動状況、運用の状況、インシデントに関する情報を主要なステークホルダーへ定期的に報告する。グローバル オペレーションや関連チームとの正確かつ迅速なコミュニケーションを徹底し、円滑な調整と迅速な対応を行う。ローカル オペレーションやシステム運用、プロダクト開発、エンジニアリングなど、複数の部門が関わる複雑なステークホルダー間の関係を調整する。
  • Safety and Performance Compliance: Actively monitor and enforce Waymo’s safety standards and performance benchmarks. Address deviations promptly to ensure consistent, reliable, and safe operations across all markets. Lead safety / risk audits and partner with safety leads to implement risk-reduction strategies./ 安全性とパフォーマンスに関するコンプライアンス: Waymo の安全基準およびパフォーマンス ベンチマークを積極的にモニタリングし、遵守を徹底する。基準からの逸脱に対しては速やかに対処し、すべての市場において運用の一貫性、信頼性、安全性を担保する。また、安全性 / リスク監査を主導し、安全監督責任者と連携してリスク軽減戦略を実行する。
  • Continuous Improvement Feedback: Proactively generate ideas to drive continuous improvement in daily operations - even if the problem is ambiguous - and take full ownership of the solution. Report recurring issues and recommend potential adjustments to procedures, collaborating with cross-functional teams to enhance operational efficiency. / 継続的な改善とフィードバック: 課題があいまいな状況であっても、日々の業務における継続的な改善を推進するため、積極的にアイデアを創出し、解決に向けてオーナーシップを持って取り組む。繰り返し発生する問題を報告し、プロセスの最適化を提案し、部門横断的なチームと連携して運用の効率化を高める。

You have:/必要な条件/経験: 

  • Minimum 6 years experience in consulting, technical operations,  product operations, technical account management,  Partner management / advocacy, or other related roles that blend strong technical know-how with stakeholder engagement to uncover problems and work cross functionally with technical teams to build solutions to those problems./ コンサルティング、技術運用、プロダクト運用、テクニカル アカウント マネジメント、パートナー管理 / アドボカシー、またはそれらに関連する領域における 6 年以上の実務経験。高度な技術的ノウハウとステークホルダーを巻き込む力を備え、課題を発見し、技術チームと部門横断的な連携によるソリューション構築をリードした経験を有すること。
  • Fluent in both English and Japanese / 英語と日本語の両方に堪能であること。
  • Proven strong data-driven decision-making skills and comfort working with relevant technologies for continuous operations. /データドリブン型の優れた意思決定能力を備え、継続的な運用のための関連技術に精通していること。
  • Cross functional collaboration experience: ability to hear issues from teams in person, translate those problems into clear problem statements, and work with global engineering teams to resolve those issues./ 部門横断的な連携経験: チームから課題を直接ヒアリングし、それらを明確な問題定義として言語化できる能力。また、グローバルのエンジニアリング チームと協力して課題を解決する能力。
  • Some flexibility in scheduling to ensure business continuity with AMER and EMEA /AMER および EMEA との事業継続を確保するための柔軟なスケジュール調整能力。

We prefer:/望ましい経験/スキル: 

  • Bachelor's degree in business, supply chain, operations research, engineering, or other technical / operational studies. / ビジネス、サプライ チェーン、オペレーションズ リサーチ、エンジニアリング、またはその他技術 / 運用研究分野における学士号
  • Experience with working with non-local product / engineering teams (especially in United States) / 海外のプロダクト / エンジニアリング チーム（特に米国拠点）との連携経験
  • 3+ years experience with real-time operations / リアルタイム オペレーションにおける 3 年以上の実務経験
  • Experience with autonomous vehicles / 自律走行車に関連する経験
  • Experience overseeing and supporting operational teams, including monitoring performance and ensuring adherence to protocols. / パフォーマンス監視やプロトコル遵守の徹底など、運用チームの監督およびサポートの経験
  • Experience directly guiding large groups of people in a real-time operational setting, providing clear instructions and ensuring performance and safety standards. / リアルタイムの運用環境において大規模なグループを直接指揮し、的確な指示を通じてパフォーマンスおよび安全基準を担保した経験

Travel: / 出張について: 

  • Onboarding: Travel to SF, USA and Phoenix USA / オンボーディング: 米国サンフランシスコおよび米国フェニックスへの出張
  • Summits: Travel to Phoenix, USA (~once or twice a year)  for a team summit / サミット: チームサミットの参加のため米国フェニックスへの出張（年に 1～2 回程度）
  • Potential travel to other markets in the EMEA region on an ad-hoc basis as we launch / scale new markets / 新規市場の立ち上げや事業拡大に伴い、EMEA 地域の各拠点への出張が発生する可能性あり

Please note that Waymo may not be able to employ remotely in all locations. Please speak with your recruiter about your preferred location for remote work when you begin the interview process. / Waymo では、採用地によってリモート勤務が適用されない場合がありますのでご了承ください。ご希望のリモート勤務場所については、選考プロセスの開始時に採用担当者までご相談ください。

title: Charging Automation Lead
location: San Francisco, CA, USA; Mountain View, CA, USA
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

In this hybrid role, you will report to a Director of Global Place Workplace & eMobility

 

You will:

  • Lead the smart buildings design and implementation of automated test scripts and standardized workflows for EV charging features, from initial development through utility energization and final customer activation.
  • Act as the primary technical lead ensuring seamless integration between vehicle mechatronic embedded controls, charging hardware, and cloud-based mobile applications.
  • Define and enforce rigorous quality standards and testing strategies, integrating automated test suites into CI/CD pipelines to ensure continuous delivery of high-quality charging infrastructure.Define and enforce rigorous quality standards and testing strategies, integrating automated test suites into CI/CD pipelines to ensure continuous delivery of high-quality charging infrastructure.
  • Identify process inefficiencies and lead implementation of automation virtualization opportunities to reduce manual testing and streamline commissioning activities.
  • Collaborate with product managers, developers, and feature owners while managing external commissioning vendors to ensure all site technical requirements are met.
  • Adhere to and enforce all safety precautions, particularly regarding high-voltage systems and on-vehicle testing, treating safety as the top priority.
  • Guide others in best practices, frameworks, and troubleshooting methodologies for complex electrification systems.

 

You have:

  • A Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Business, or a related technical field.

  • 5+ years of relevant experience with automation/scripting, specifically with high proficiency in Python and experience with frameworks like Autobot or similar.

  • Extensive technical experience with electrification features, EV charging infrastructure deployment, and the commissioning lifecycle.
  • Strong understanding of CI/CD pipelines and tools such as GitHub, Jenkins, or Azure DevOps, along with version control systems like Git.
  • Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams and manage complex projects that involve both hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) bench work and in-vehicle testing.
  • Demonstrated ability to communicate technical issues, project status, and strategic recommendations to leadership and external partners.

 

We prefer:

  • A Master’s degree or PhD in Engineering or a Master’s in Business Administration (MBA).
  • 8+ years of test automation experience with a focus on web/mobile applications and vehicle-to-grid integration.
  • Hands-on experience with automotive diagnostic tools such as INCA, MDA, CANalyzer, or Vehicle Spy.
  • Understanding of Vehicle Electrical Architecture (e.g., VIP, SDV) and serial data buses including LIN, CAN, GMLAN, and Ethernet.
  • High voltage or electrified vehicle safety training and hands-on experience with on-vehicle serial protocols.
  • Certification in Quality Assurance and/or Test Automation (e.g., Six Sigma, ISTQB) and experience with JIRA/Problem Resolution Tracking Systems (PRTS).

 

Travel Arrangements:

  • This role requires travel approximately 25% of the time. Specifically, the employee will be expected to travel quarterly as part of their job responsibilities.

 

The expected base salary range for this full-time position across US locations is listed below. Actual starting pay will be based on job-related factors, including exact work location, experience, relevant training and education, and skill level. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the role location or, if the role can be performed remote, the specific salary range for your preferred location, during the hiring process. 

Waymo employees are also eligible to participate in Waymo’s discretionary annual bonus program, equity incentive plan, and generous Company benefits program, subject to eligibility requirements. 

Salary Range
$152,000—$187,000 USD

title: Charging Infrastructure Program Manager
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

Our vehicles drive themselves, but you will be the driving force behind our charging infrastructure strategy. In this high-impact, strategic role, you will oversee the rapid, high-volume deployment of EV charging infrastructure while providing end-to-end operational oversight for our network. You will establish a scaled, repeatable process for the entire deployment lifecycle—including site selection, design, and construction—while managing ongoing asset maintenance, energy optimization, and lifecycle planning.

In this hybrid role you will report to the Director of Global Workplace & eMobility

 

You will:

  • End-to-End Infrastructure Deployment: Oversee the planning, design, procurement, and construction delivery of EV charging stations (including DC Fast Chargers) across multiple sites, ensuring adherence to project schedules and organizational standards
  • eMobility Strategy & Sustainability: Define the comprehensive eMobility strategy, including targets for fleet electrification and charging network expansion. Partner with Sustainability teams to implement renewable energy systems and achieve carbon-free goals
  • Technical Operations & Maintenance: Manage ongoing maintenance, asset repair, and hardware/software performance evaluations. Prioritize critical repairs to achieve 99% charger uptime and oversee the 24/7 call center team supporting EV charging service tickets
  • Financial & Budget Management: Develop and manage annual CapEx and OpEx budgets for all eMobility initiatives. Maximize ROI by securing funding and leveraging relevant eMobility incentives, grants, and tax credits.
  • Stakeholder & Vendor Management: Act as the primary liaison with utility companies, government agencies, and internal cross-functional partners (e.g., Facilities, IT, Finance, Legal). Oversee vendor teams responsible for project delivery, maintenance programs, and financing
  • Reporting, Policy, & Compliance: Monitor industry trends, relevant policies, and electrical standards. Establish KPIs, analyze charging utilization data, and create comprehensive reports for executive leadership to measure environmental impact and system performance

 

You have:

title: Data Scientist
location: Mountain View, California, USA; San Francisco, California, USA
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

Rigorous performance evaluation of the Waymo Driver is a critical part of scaling our ride hailing service and achieving Waymo’s audacious goals. Waymo data scientists work hand-in-hand with engineering teams at each stage of the software development cycle, employing statistical models and developing metrics and measurement frameworks to ensure that the Waymo Driver meets our strict standards for safety, compliance, and driving and service quality. Autonomous driving presents a new paradigm in data science: in addition to leveraging data collected on-road, we generate our own data using state-of-the-art simulation technology—resulting in denser signals and challenging new problems in estimation and experimental design.

In this hybrid role you will report to a Data Science Manager.

You will:

  • Develop evaluation frameworks for autonomous vehicle performance, for large-scale ML models, and for the quality of simulation.
  • Develop new metrics, interpret trends, and investigate anomalies in data from simulation and on-road driving.
  • Develop novel statistical methods to handle unique aspects of AV data; e.g. rate estimation with rare events, combining real and synthetic data, etc.
  • Frame and solve ambiguous problems, derive data-driven conclusions, and communicate findings to senior stakeholders.
  • Collaborate with Product and Engineering partners developing the Waymo Driver and Waymo’s simulation software; facilitate deployment readiness decisions for both products.

You have:

  • Degree in a quantitative field (e.g. Statistics, Mathematics, Physics)
  • 3+ years of industry experience solving data science problems or a PhD in a quantitative field
  • Expertise using advanced statistical methods in an applied setting; familiarity with ML systems/models
  • Demonstrated knowledge of Python/SQL/R data analysis libraries and packages

We prefer:

  • PhD in a quantitative field
  • Experience solving problems related to Autonomous Driving or Ride Hailing
  • Experience in adjacent relevant areas like Advanced Machine Learning (Deep Learning and Diffusion models), Traffic Modeling, Safety Evaluation or Prediction

title: Demand Operations Manager  - San Francisco
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

Waymo Operations exists to deliver the Waymo Driver to the world. We are a global team building and scaling the world's first and leading autonomous fleet and operations platform. From component sourcing to end customer management, we enable and create value for Waymo through scaled and orchestrated deployment of the Waymo Driver. At Waymo, we are dedicated to building a culture that promotes collaboration and celebration. We value our team members' unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences and support and encourage all team members to share their ideas to help Waymo better serve the communities in which we operate.

As the Demand Operations Manager, you will sit at the intersection of fleet technology, rider experience, and urban diplomacy. Your mission is to scale the Waymo Driver across multiple markets by leveraging sophisticated internal tools to harmonize fleet supply with rider demand. You will be the primary architect of our "ground truth" strategy ensuring that our autonomous fleet is pre-positioned effectively, that pickup and drop-off (PUDO) performance is seamless, and that our presence in the community is viewed as a net positive. This role requires a unique blend of operational rigor, data-driven decision-making, and the ability to consult with internal and external stakeholders to de-risk our expansion while maximizing rider satisfaction.

In this role, you will report to a reports to Market Operations Lead

You will:

  • Rider Experience: Monitor, mitigate, and respond to service quality issues based on data, rider, and community feedback to ensure a superior and seamless service environment.

  • Strategic Pre-positioning: Utilize internal systems and forecasting tools to oversee fleet staging, ensuring vehicles are positioned to meet real-time rider demand and minimize wait times across diverse territories.

  • PUDO Performance: Own the end-to-end Pickup and Drop-off experience at top venues. Analyze performance data to identify friction points, implement operational fixes that improve safety and efficiency at the curb, and work with external partners to enhance the experience.

  • Event Management: Lead the operational planning for high-traffic city events, creating specialized staging and PUDO strategies that prioritize rider experience while adhering to local regulations.

  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with internal teams (i.e. Product, Public Affairs, Mapping) to ensure that our technical capabilities align with the localized needs of the communities we serve.

  • Community Integration & Risk Mitigation: Partner with Marketing, Public Affairs, and local stakeholders to establish high-impact relationships that proactively de-risk negative operational impacts while positioning the Waymo Driver as a safe, reliable, and considerate neighbor.

You have:

  • Experience: Bachelor’s degree plus 5+ years of experience in operations management, urban planning, consulting, or logistics, with a focus on demand-side optimization or fleet management.

  • Market Scaling: Proven track record of managing operations across multiple markets or complex urban environments.

  • Stakeholder Management: Exceptional ability to negotiate and consult with external partners, including city officials, event organizers, and community leaders.

We prefer:

  • Industry Background: Experience in the Ride-sharing, Autonomous Vehicle (AV) space or high-growth TNC (Transportation Network Company) industry.

  • Urban Diplomacy: Background in urban design, transit planning, or public policy with a focus on mitigating community impact in high-density areas.

  • Management Consulting Background: Prior experience in management consulting or strategic operational consulting, with a focus on structured problem-solving and navigating ambiguous environments.

  • Large-Scale Event Experience: Proven ability to manage high-stakes logistics for major city events (sports, festivals, political summits).

  • Ride-Sharing Experience & Market Health Monitoring: Proven expertise in optimizing ride-sharing customer experiences by leveraging advanced data analytics to monitor market health, identify demand trends, and implement proactive service quality interventions.

Travel required:

  • 10%

In this hybrid role, you will report to the Head of Ride Hail Operations.

The Market Ops Enablement Lead is responsible for establishing the operational foundation that enables scalable, efficient, and cost effective supply across Waymo. This role defines the standards, frameworks, and readiness models required to ensure the fleet is positioned, available, and optimized to meet customer demand while maintaining the highest levels of safety, reliability, and service quality.

This role is primarily accountable for supply strategy and fleet optimization, ensuring that vehicle availability, utilization, and readiness are aligned with real-time and forecasted demand. This includes defining how markets plan, deploy, and manage supply; standardizing operating rhythms across supply, demand, and fleet readiness; and enabling partners to execute consistently against these expectations. The Market Ops Enablement Lead ensures that every market is equipped with the tools, processes, and insights needed to maximize commercial miles, minimize downtime, and deliver a seamless rider experience. Infrastructure, energy, and depot planning are treated as enablers of supply performance, ensuring that charging, maintenance, and depot operations support, not constrain, fleet availability. This role partners cross-functionally to ensure these capabilities are aligned with supply needs, but remains focused on driving end-to-end supply effectiveness and operational execution.

Serving as the voice of operations, this role translates frontline insights and performance data into clear, scalable standards that improve how supply is planned, deployed, and managed across the network. The Market Ops Enablement Lead plays a critical role in enabling partner success, driving consistency across markets, and ensuring the organization can scale efficiently while maintaining operational excellence.

Success in this role is measured by fleet availability, supply-demand balance, utilization, cost, service reliability, and the organization’s ability to scale supply efficiently while maintaining a high-quality rider experience.

 

You will:

  • Develop and maintain supply-focused operating standards, frameworks, and documentation that define how fleet availability, deployment, and utilization are planned and executed across markets
  • Establish and manage supply readiness assessments for market launches, partner onboarding, and major operational changes, ensuring fleet readiness aligns with demand from day one
  • Define and implement supply planning and execution rhythms that align fleet availability, and utilization 
  • Design and standardize fleet readiness and operational processes that minimize downtime and maximize vehicle availability, ensuring the fleet captures every available commercial mile
  • Partner with infrastructure, energy, and depot teams to ensure charging, maintenance, and depot operations enable and do not constrain supply performance
  • Translate frontline operational insights, supply-demand imbalances, and customer signals into actionable improvements to supply strategy, deployment, and execution
  • Build and maintain closed-loop feedback mechanisms to continuously refine supply models, fleet readiness processes, and market execution standards
  • Lead cross-market reviews to identify trends in utilization, availability, and supply gaps, driving systemic improvements and scalability
  • Support markets through launches and transitions, ensuring supply models, fleet readiness, and operational execution are validated and sustainable
  • Monitor and analyze fleet availability, utilization, supply-demand balance, and service performance metrics to ensure standards are delivering intended outcomes
  • Continuously evolve the supply operating model to improve efficiency, scalability, and reliability while maintaining a high-quality rider experience

You have:

  • 15+ years in operations leadership, infrastructure planning, or program management within AVs, ride-hailing, mobility, logistics, or energy/infrastructure operations
  • Experience designing operational frameworks and execution models for multi-market services
  • Experience working across infrastructure, utilities, or energy planning to support operational fleet readiness
  • Ability to represent frontline operational needs to product, engineering, infrastructure, and strategy stakeholders
  • Proven track record of integrating customer experience, reliability, and fleet performance into operational standards
  • Strong analytical and systems thinking skills to balance operational demand with infrastructure capacity
  • Proficiency using operational and infrastructure data trends to drive continuous improvement and operational maturity
  • Ability to balance global consistency with local regulatory, infrastructure, and partner requirements

title: Director of Engineering, London Site Lead
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

The Simulator Team at Waymo builds state-of-the-art simulations of realistic environments for evaluating, training and scaling the Waymo Driver. Your team will be a diverse, and collaborative group of machine learning (ML) engineers, software engineers and data scientists. We develop industry-leading simulation solutions using advanced ML algorithms that measure and enhance the performance of the Waymo Driver. We achieve those goals by jointly modeling the real world, including realistic agents (vehicles, pedestrians, cyclists, motorcyclists), static road elements, traffic control systems, weather conditions, and the full sensor suite including camera, lidar and radars.

We enhance our simulations' fidelity, scalability, and controllability by leveraging state of the art machine learning. This includes large language models, foundational world models, and reconstructive methods, all trained on datasets using both generative and reconstructive AI, alongside traditional rendering techniques.

In this hybrid role, you will report to the Director of Engineering, Simulator.


You will:

  • Serve as the connective tissue for the London office, ensuring that engineers across Sim Realism, DUE, AIF, and other functions feel part of a single, cohesive "One Waymo" site.
  • Act as the primary executive conduit between Waymo's Foundation Model initiatives and Google DeepMind teams in the UK to accelerate joint R&D.
  • Define the technical roadmap for the London Sim Realism team, driving tight alignment with Waymo's overarching simulation and AI-first objectives.
  • Direct the research and engineering of cutting-edge generative models for sensor-level and semantic-level simulation (Waymo World Model).
  • Recruit, manage, and mentor a top-tier Sim Realism engineering organization, attracting elite talent in machine learning, generative AI, and simulation.
  • Partner closely with Real Estate & Workplace Services (REWS) to design and execute a site strategy that accommodates aggressive team growth.

 

You have:

  • A PhD or Master's degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Robotics, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).
  • A proven track record of leading, scaling, and managing large engineering organizations in a fast-paced, research-driven, and highly technical environment.
  • Deep foundational knowledge and applied expertise in machine learning, generative foundation models, and/or complex simulation environments.
  • Demonstrated ability to drive complex, multi-year, cross-functional projects from early conception to global impact.
  • Extensive experience managing and developing senior engineers, staff engineers, and technical leads/managers.

 

We prefer:

  • Familiarity with autonomous vehicle technology, robotics, or complex embodied AI systems.
  • Exceptional executive communication skills, with a proven ability to build consensus and influence stakeholders across reporting lines.
  • Demonstrated capability to translate highly ambiguous, long-term strategic goals into focused, actionable engineering roadmaps.

You will:

  • Develop and execute comprehensive board-level and module-level validation test plans for Waymo hardware, by working closely with EE and software teams
  • Execute electrical validation test for silicon, board and system, covering high-speed / low-speed interfaces, voltage regulators, power delivery network (PDN), cover thermal and voltage corners.

  • Clearly document validation results. Keep track of bugs encountered and fixed. Clearly communicate test plans and results
  • Support hardware bring-up, debug, and troubleshooting
  • Enhance validation efficiency and scalability through the development of scripts and automation tools

  • Excellent communication and project management skills to work with cross functional teams and external suppliers
  • Experience executing electrical validation on the bench, including the use of: oscilloscopes, DMM, e-load, power supplies, power analyzers, spectrum analyzers, signal generators, and thermal chambers

We prefer:

  • Familiarity with relevant high-speed and low-speed communication protocols (e.g., PCIe, ethernet, I2C, SPI). Demonstrated experience validating and debugging high-speed interfaces (e.g., PCIe, DDR, MIPI, Ethernet) and low-speed interfaces (e.g., I2C, SPI, GPIO).

  • Extensive electrical debug experience
  • Experience working in a Linux environment. Familiar with scripting and automation

title: Emergency Services Liaison, Japan & Asia / 緊急サービス・リエゾン (日本・アジア担当)
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

The Waymo Safety team works to promote and help to continuously improve the safety of Waymo's fully autonomous driving technology. Our experts develop safety goals and strategies, and conduct safety engineering analyses to ensure safety is being considered throughout the design and development of our vehicles. The team develops and promotes safety strategies and policies for autonomous vehicles for its work with regulatory authorities, lawmakers, law enforcement and public and non-profit organizations.

Waymo Safety チームは、Waymo の完全自動運転技術の安全性の向上と継続的改善に取り組んでいます。Waymo の専門家は、安全目標と戦略を策定するとともに、安全工学分析を実施し、車両の設計および開発のすべての工程で安全性が考慮されるよう取り組んでいます。チームは、規制当局、立法機関、法執行機関、公共団体、非営利団体と連携するため、自動運転車に関する安全戦略およびポリシーの策定と推進を行っています。

In this role you will be hybrid based in Tokyo and report into our Head of Resilience and Incident Management.

この役割はロンドンを拠点とするハイブリッド勤務となり、レジリエンスおよびインシデント管理責任者にレポートします。 

 

You will / 業務内容

  • Be a native Japanese speaker, also proficient in English. / 日本語を母語とし、英語にも堪能であること
  • Establish and maintain primary contact with Emergency Services at management and executive levels in current and future areas of operation. / 現在および将来のオペレーション領域において、管理職および経営幹部レベルの緊急サービスとの主な連絡窓口を確立し、維持します。
  • Design, present, and certify additional Emergency Services Ambassadors (ESAs) and Emergency Services stakeholders on Waymo's mission, technology, and industry concerns. / Waymo の使命、テクノロジー、業界の懸念事項について、追加の緊急サービス アンバサダー（ESA）および緊急サービス関係者の設計、提示、認定を行います。
  • Provide oversight, review, and guidance to Emergency Services training teams and act as the final source of truth for Emergency Services questions. / 緊急サービス トレーニング チームに対して監督、レビュー、指導を行い、緊急サービスに関する質問について最終的な公式見解を示す責任を担います。
  • Represent Waymo at Emergency Services events, panels, and industry events. / 緊急サービス イベント、パネル ディスカッション、業界イベントで Waymo を代表します。
  • Act as Waymo’s on the ground representative during emergency events and coordinate with local emergency services and local authorities. / 緊急事態発生時に Waymo の現地代表として活動し、地域の緊急サービスおよび地方当局と連携します。

 

You have / 必須条件:

  • 10+ years of service in Japan’s emergency services (Police, Fire & Rescue, or Ambulance Service) / 日本の緊急サービス（警察、消防および救助、または救急サービス）での 10 年以上の勤務経験
  • Deep understanding of Japan’s legal frameworks, emergency protocols, and community engagement strategies. / 日本の法的枠組み、緊急時対応プロトコル、コミュニティ関与戦略に関する深い理解
  • Strong communication and presentation skills, capable of engaging diverse audiences. / 多様な聴衆を惹きつけることのできる、優れたコミュニケーション能力およびプレゼンテーション能力
  • A strong understanding of the specific nuances and sensitivities of Emergency Services communities in Japan. / 日本の緊急サービス コミュニティ特有のニュアンスや配慮を深く理解していること
  • The ability to establish and maintain a professional presence at executive and command staff levels. / 経営幹部および指揮スタッフレベルにおいて、専門的な信頼関係を確立し、維持できる能力
  • A collaborative mindset and the ability to work effectively with legal and other internal teams. / 協調的な姿勢を有し、法務チームやその他の社内チームと効果的に連携できる能力

 

We prefer / 歓迎条件:

  • At least 7 years in a leadership or management position within one of Japan’s principal emergency services. / 日本の主要な緊急サービス機関のいずれかにおいて、指導的立場または管理職として少なくとも 7 年の実務経験
  • Experience establishing organizational presence in new geographic locations. / 新しい地域において組織のプレゼンスを確立した経験
  • Familiarity with autonomous vehicle technology and its implications for emergency services. / 自動運転車技術およびそれが緊急サービスに与える影響に関する知識
  • A track record of policy development related to emergency management. / 緊急事態管理に関する政策立案の実績
  • Experience in a role requiring significant public speaking and stakeholder engagement. / 重要な公の場での発言や利害関係者との関与を必要とする職務の経験
  • Experience working in rapidly scaling and complex operational environments. / 急速に拡大する複雑な運用環境での業務経験

 

 Travel Requirements / 出張要件:

  • ~20% travel (Domestic & International) / 約 20% の出張（国内および海外）

 

Please Note: This role works with sensitive content or situations and may be exposed to graphic, controversial, and/or upsetting topics or content. / 注: この職務ではデリケートなコンテンツや状況を扱うことがあり、刺激的、物議を醸す、または不快感を伴うトピックやコンテンツに接する可能性があります。

title: Engineering & Hardware Category Manager
location: Mountain View, CA, USA; Phoenix, AZ, USA
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

Waymo's Procurement and Vendor Enablement Office (PVEO) is the strategic engine behind our rapid commercial scaling. We don't just buy services; we design the workforce architecture that powers the Waymo Driver. In this senior leadership role, you will lead the Enterprise Outsourcing & Services vertical. You will partner with Executives across Engineering, Operations, and Corporate functions to define the "Future of Work" at Waymo—determining how we leverage the global external ecosystem to scale our Mapping, Labeling, Engineering, and Corporate capabilities.

You will:

  • Develop and execute a comprehensive procurement strategy for Engineering Services and R&D Hardware, balancing cost optimization with speed-to-market for prototypes and technical development.
  • Proactively manage a high-volume pipeline of outsourced engineering workforce contracts; lead negotiations for mission-critical engineering design firms to secure favorable long-term commercial and IP terms.
  • Structure and negotiate complex Statements of Work (SOWs) and Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) agreements for hardware development to ensure successful project delivery and ROI.
  • Optimize Joint Design Manufacturing (JDM) and Original Design Manufacturing (ODM) relationships for R&D components, ensuring competitive pricing and seamless fulfillment logistics for hardware iterations.
  • Act as the trusted advisor to Engineering and Hardware leadership; lead complex "make vs. buy" analyses and influence technical requirements to drive commercial value and mitigate supply chain risk.
  • Partner with Legal (IP/Security) to ensure all engineering services and hardware agreements meet Waymo’s rigorous intellectual property protection and cybersecurity standards.

You have:

  • 7+ years of strategic procurement experience, with at least 4+ years dedicated to Engineering Services, R&D, or Hardware sourcing.
  • Deep expertise in outsourced engineering models (managed services, staff augmentation, fixed-bid) and experience negotiating with Tier 1 engineering service providers.
  • Proven ability to draft and negotiate complex commercial contracts, including MSAs, NREs, JDM/ODM agreements, and highly technical Professional Services SOWs.
  • Experience managing R&D hardware lifecycles (EVT, DVT, PVT) and negotiating with custom component manufacturers and prototyping houses.
  • Strong financial acumen with the ability to present TCO (Total Cost of Ownership) analysis to Finance and Hardware Engineering leadership.
  • Ability to operate in a high-velocity environment, managing multiple concurrent high-priority sourcing events for unreleased technologies.

We prefer:

  • Experience in a hyper-growth Tech, Autonomous Vehicle (AV), or automotive manufacturing environment.
  • Engineering background or profound understanding of hardware development and systems engineering principles.
  • Experience using Ariba P2P, e-sourcing, and Spend Analysis tools.

Travel: 

  • Estimated 10-15% (Vendor QBRs, supplier audits, & HQ visits as needed).

title: Engineering Manager, Rider Growth
location: Mountain View, CA, USA; San Francisco, CA, USA
description_text:
Waymo is an autonomous driving technology company with the mission to be the world's most trusted driver. Since its start as the Google Self-Driving Car Project in 2009, Waymo has focused on building the Waymo Driver—The World's Most Experienced Driver™—to improve access to mobility while saving thousands of lives now lost to traffic crashes. The Waymo Driver powers Waymo’s fully autonomous ride-hail service and can also be applied to a range of vehicle platforms and product use cases. The Waymo Driver has provided over ten million rider-only trips, enabled by its experience autonomously driving over 100 million miles on public roads and tens of billions in simulation across 15+ U.S. states.

As the Engineering Manager for Rider Growth, you will build, lead, and inspire a high-performing, cross-functional engineering squad (comprising backend, full-stack, and mobile engineers) focused on driving sustainable rider acquisition, activation, retention, and lifetime value (LTV).

The team will build features that educate and onboard new riders to driverless technology, design subscription and loyalty programs that drive daily commuter habits, and leverage machine learning and economics to optimize dynamic promotions that shape passenger demand to match fleet availability.

You will:

  •  Recruit, mentor, and grow a diverse, inclusive, and highly skilled engineering team. Foster a culture of high ownership, rapid iteration, and technical craftsmanship.

  • Partner with Product Management, User Experience (UX) Design, and Data Science, define the strategy and execute a roadmap aimed at accelerating rider growth and retention.

  • Lead the technical strategy for rider onboarding, focusing on shrinking the time-to-value (TTV) from app download to the first driverless trip. Build smooth UX flows that solve high cognitive trust barriers, educate riders on vehicle safety, and optimize physical pickup/drop-off logistics.

  • Architect and scale rider retention frameworks, including subscription services, commuter packages, and loyalty programs that incentivize frequent, habitual riding.

  • Collaborate with ML scientists and economists to design and scale highly reliable, low-latency promotional engines. Build backend systems that distribute personalized, budget-capped promotions to shape rider demand and maximize incremental ridership.

  • Champion a highly quantitative, data-driven engineering culture. Drive standard-setting experimentation practices, ensuring the team runs robust A/B tests, interprets statistical outcomes accurately, and rapidly integrates learnings back into the product.

  • Oversee the scalability, latency, and reliability of your team's services. Ensure seamless, high-performance integrations with Waymo's core routing, fleet dispatch, pricing engines, and billing ledgers.

You have:

  • 4+ years of experience directly managing and scaling high-performing software engineering teams, with a strong track record of mentoring senior and staff-level engineers.

  • 6+ years of hands-on software development experience building highly scalable, distributed backend systems (Go, Java, C++, or Python) and/or high-performance B2C mobile applications (iOS/Android).

  • Deep, practical experience running systematic product experiments (A/B testing, multivariate testing) with a strong grasp of statistical significance, experimental design, and cohort analysis.

  • Fluency in using data analytics tools (SQL, Python/Pandas, Tableau, etc.) to diagnose user drops, analyze funnel health, and identify new conversion opportunities.

  • The ability to thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment, managing multiple parallel product experiments while maintaining absolute engineering craftsmanship.

We prefer:

  • Extensive experience scaling B2C products by driving measurable improvements across the Acquisition, Retention, and LTV (Lifetime Value) lifecycle.

  • Proven success architecting ML-driven systems for real-time offer surfacing and ranking, leveraging techniques like Multi-Armed Bandits to optimize incentives at scale.

  • Outstanding communication and collaboration skills. You can seamlessly translate high-level business and economic goals into rigorous technical requirements and align stakeholders across the business.
